title: Good: lichen_l2.c ownership comments on TX/RX packet handling
description:
Locations: lichen_l2.c:148-152, 628-634

The comments documenting Zephyr net_pkt ownership semantics are valuable:
- TX (line 148-152): 'On success (return 0), L2 takes ownership... On error (return < 0), caller retains ownership'
- RX (line 628-634): 'On success (ret >= 0): net_recv_data takes ownership... We MUST NOT access or unref pkt after this point'

Why this is good: Packet ownership in Zephyr's net stack is a common source of double-free and use-after-free bugs. These comments make the contract explicit and prevent 'helpful' refactors that add pkt access after handoff. Worth defending if someone suggests removing 'obvious' comments.
